findings.problem: Lucien's private-scene dialogue slips into psychologizing aphorism and one unnecessary full-form construction ("I would like"), which breaks the style-bible brief for a moderate-contraction, sensuous-but-plain schoolmaster. In the middle of an exhausted quarry-floor encounter, these lines sound composed from above rather than spoken under strain.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:343, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:351, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:409, replace the abstract diagnosis and maxim with language tied to the immediate room: breath, shoulder pain, the false name, fear, the wall, the cough, or the fact of choosing each other anyway. Keep Lucien's dry wit, but make him plainer under stress and use contractions unless emphasis is doing real work.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, at the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:343, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:351, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:409, Lucien uses contractions in at least two of the three lines and each line names a concrete present fact in the room or between the bodies rather than offering a generalized diagnosis or maxim. FAIL if any of those beats still reads like therapist talk or a quotable aphorism.

findings.problem: The chapter reuses the same paired-negation descriptor shape three times in pivotal moments ("not warm, not cold..." / "not gentle, not rough..." / "not pushing, not restraining..."). What reads as controlled precision once becomes a visible prose tic by the third use, flattening the chapter's strongest emotional beats into the same sentence music.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast at least two of the sentences anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:55, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:321, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:333 into positive, scene-specific description. Name what Lucien's look or hand actually does in those beats instead of defining it by what it is not.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than one of the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:55, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:321, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:333 keeps the paired-negation frame, and at least two of the three lines use affirmative, concrete description tied to the immediate action or sensation. FAIL if two or more of those beats still rely on the same "not X, not Y" construction.

summary: Chapter 10 lands its causal obligations: the press move is materially convincing, the intimacy scene is rendered on-page rather than sanitized, continuity holds, and the narration stays in canonical past tense. The unresolved failures are local but visible: Lucien's quarry-floor intimacy dialogue turns too authored and aphoristic in a few key beats, and a paired-negation description formula repeats until it reads as a tic.
